decks.notes: This deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of aggressive Goblin creatures, utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Ringleader to search for key pieces and maintain card advantage. The deck also includes powerful tribal synergy with cards like Goblin Warchief and Goblin King to boost the effectiveness of the Goblin creatures. With the ability to disrupt the opponent's mana base using cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port, the deck can quickly close out games with cards like Siege-Gang Commander and Gempalm Incinerator.

This deck aims to quickly assemble the combo of Hermit Druid with a Necrotic Ooze in the graveyard, allowing for a game-winning loop of self-mill and creature reanimation. The key is to efficiently get Hermit Druid into play and activate its ability, filling the graveyard with creatures to fuel the combo. Various cards like Unearth, Animate Dead, and Karmic Guide help with reanimation, while Gemstone Mine and Birds of Paradise provide the necessary mana acceleration. The deck is designed to quickly set up the combo and overwhelm the opponent with a powerful board presence.

This deck focuses on deploying efficient, aggressive black creatures while disrupting the opponent's hand and board with targeted discard spells. It aims to quickly deplete the opponent's resources and overwhelm them with strong creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Graveborn Muse. Mishra's Factory provides an additional threat, while Cursed Scroll can be used for direct damage. Wasteland is included to disrupt the opponent's mana base. Overall, the deck is designed to apply constant pressure and maintain control of the game.

The main strategy of this deck revolves around locking down the opponent's board with Stasis, while using Counterspells and other disruption to protect the combo. The deck aims to control the game by denying the opponent resources and disrupting their game plan, while setting up a win condition through card advantage and burn spells. It heavily relies on card draw and efficient mana usage to maintain its lock on the game and ultimately outlast the opponent.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the pieces needed to cast Replenish, bringing back powerful enchantments such as Opalescence and Parallax Wave from the graveyard. By utilizing cards like Attunement and Intuition to stock the graveyard and find key pieces, the deck can lock down the opponent with a combination of enchantment removal, creature removal, and a strong board presence. The deck aims to control the game while setting up for a game-winning combo to overwhelm the opponent.

The main focus of the RG Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm opponents with aggressive creatures and burn spells. The deck aims to apply consistent pressure and deal damage to the opponent as quickly as possible, utilizing efficient creatures like Quirion Dryad and Mogg Fanatic, along with powerful burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Fireblast. The inclusion of cards like Rancor and Sylvan Library help provide additional aggression and card advantage to maintain a strong board presence. The deck is designed to win the game swiftly by dealing damage early and often.

The main focus of this deck is to use the Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle combo to quickly get a powerful creature on the board while also disrupting the opponent's strategy with counterspells and control cards. The deck is designed to be efficient and aggressive, utilizing cards like Misdirection, Gush, and Foil to maintain tempo and card advantage. The inclusion of cards like Opt and Portent help to dig for key pieces of the combo while Mishra's Factory provides additional utility and card advantage. The overall strategy revolves around quickly assembling the combo and outpacing the opponent.

This deck focuses on quickly assembling the Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle combo to create a powerful threat while disrupting the opponent's game plan with counterspells and Stifle effects. The deck also includes card draw and selection spells to find the combo pieces efficiently. With the ability to protect the combo with counterspells and Stifle effects, the deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and win the game through a quick and powerful combination of cards.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle utilizing cheap and efficient burn spells, along with early creature damage and direct damage. By consistently applying pressure and maximizing damage output, the deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total to zero before they are able to stabilize or mount a significant defense. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Black Vise and Pyrostatic Pillar to further punish the opponent and prevent them from gaining a foothold in the game.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill up your graveyard with enchantments, then use Replenish to bring them all back into play at once. This combo is supported by cards like Parallax Wave and Opalescence, which interact in powerful ways with each other and other enchantments in the deck. The deck also includes counterspells and removal spells to help protect the combo and disrupt your opponent's game plan. Overall, the key strategy of this deck is to overwhelm your opponent with a powerful enchantment-based combo.

The deck focuses on assembling the Phyrexian Dreadnought combo by using various card draw and manipulation spells to find the necessary pieces quickly. Stifle is utilized to protect the combo and disrupt opponent's strategies. Counterspells and other interaction spells are included to control the game and ensure the combo can go off smoothly. The deck aims to quickly set up and execute the combo while keeping opponents off balance with disruptive spells.

This deck utilizes the Oath of Druids strategy to control the board and disrupt opponent's plays. With a land destruction package including Wasteland and Rishadan Port, as well as Sphere of Resistance to slow down opponents, the goal is to establish a dominant position on the battlefield. Additional support comes from cards like Sylvan Library for card advantage and Swords to Plowshares for removal. The win condition revolves around finishing off opponents with creature threats like Terravore and Mishra's Factory, while maintaining a strong defensive stance with cards like Winter's Grasp.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and efficiently assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful threat. The deck includes a mix of countermagic, card draw, and disruption to protect the combo and control the game. With the ability to potentially win the game on turn two, this deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver opponents in the early stages of the game. By effectively leveraging the Stiflenought combo, the deck seeks to establish a dominant board presence and secure victory.

This deck focuses on quickly putting pressure on the opponent by playing aggressive creatures like Quirion Dryad, Jackal Pup, and Call of the Herd, supported by efficient burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Incinerate. The goal is to deal damage quickly and efficiently, using cards like Grim Lavamancer and Lava Dart to control the board and keep the pressure on. The inclusion of cards like Naturalize and Rancor provide additional flexibility and power to the deck, while cards like Sylvan Library help to ensure consistent draws and maintain tempo.

The main strategy of this deck is to quickly flood the board with Goblin creatures, utilizing their synergies and abilities to deal fast and consistent damage to the opponent. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creature plays and land disruption, while also having some removal options to deal with threats. The deck's focus on aggression and resource denial makes it a powerful force in the Premodern 1 vs 1 format, where speed and efficiency are key to victory.

The main concept of this Enchantress combo deck is to quickly accumulate a large number of enchantments on the battlefield, particularly through cards like Argothian Enchantress, Enchantress's Presence, and Mirri's Guile. This allows the player to generate a significant amount of card advantage and mana using Serra's Sanctum. The deck aims to set up a lock with cards like Sterling Grove and Solitary Confinement, eventually winning the game by either overwhelming the opponent with a massive board state or by utilizing the Replenish card to bring back a large number of enchantments from the graveyard.
